verdict: Green; consolidate shared legend-block schema walk into a private transformations helper

title: Semantic Boundary Review
verdict: YELLOW → GREEN after FTR-FUNCTIONAL-SEMANTIC-MODEL-SPLIT-P5 implementation
project_takeaway: The Functional/Semantic Model boundary was misplaced in identifiable, bounded places (chapters 03 and 10). A minimal navigational recut was sufficient; recursive vocabulary growth was already braked by the Adoption Strategy chapter, not by the boundary itself.

title: Functional/Semantic Boundary Independent Assessment
verdict: YELLOW — recut plausible but over-justified; consumer evidence points at projection ergonomics and column-role contract, not model boundary work; Technical Model placeholders are the real structural gap
project_takeaway: The recut delivered read-path clarity but not contract hygiene. Consumer evidence from Adoption-001 and the dynamic projection analysis ranked projection management and column-role semantics as the primary lever. The Technical Model backbone (ch01_structure, ch02_contracts) remained the unfilled gap.

title: Canonical Meta Boundary Assessment
verdict: Architectural problem real and medium-sized; E2-narrow repair confirmed (prune legend_blocks[*].resolved, xref_crosstable[*].dense_axes.resolved, xref_crosstable[*].column_keys); addendum developed Intent-vs-Resolution as the more stable boundary rule
project_takeaway: The persistence boundary should preserve declarative intent and discard computed resolution. Intent-vs-Resolution is a more stable rule than Canonical-vs-Carrier because it focuses on the nature of the value, not on which producer wrote it — allowing a new producer to apply the rule locally without consulting the registry.

title: Meta Flattening Hypothesis
verdict: orientation artifact — not a finding-based review; proposes meta-flattening direction: less central meta theory, more feature-local contracts; FK Helpers as pilot; code-first falsification before cleanup
project_takeaway: _meta should be treated as a carrier, not a central ontology. Feature semantics should be owned by feature-local contracts. Global metadata categories should be introduced only when several features prove the shared abstraction reduces complexity. Default risk balance: simplify legacy metadata unless current code proves the layer is still needed.

title: Review 005: Domain Contract Registry and Metadata Reference Model
focus: domain-contract registry, metadata-reference propagation, registry control loop
Verdict: Closed after Slice 1d; control-loop mode proven and Review 005 implementation work paused.
verdict: closed_after_slice_1d
project_takeaway: Use the proven review -> narrow slice -> code trace -> registry declaration -> guard/test -> gap disposition loop, but do not continue domain-contract/meta-reference registry expansion by default.

title: Review 006: Consumer Pipeline Operability and Maintainer Autonomy
focus: consumer pipeline operability, plugin reduction, maintainer autonomy
Verdict: Complete; standard consumers remain maintainable, with rules_path as the first narrow operability slice.
verdict: complete_rules_path_first
project_takeaway: Improve maintainer autonomy through small pipeline operability slices, starting with validate_references.rules_path rather than a broad config DSL or plugin purge.

title: Review 007: Domain Intent vs Render / Adapter Boundary and Meta Simplification
focus: domain intent, render/adapter boundary, parser canonical meta writes
Verdict: Complete; do not start adapter/render implementation work before meta simplification guardrails.
verdict: complete_guard_before_adapter_work
project_takeaway: Protect the parser canonical-write boundary first, preserve the four presentation families as persistent payload, and defer adapter/render rewrites until the meta boundary is documented and guarded.
